title: Grey Goo
notes: Game itself is stable, but cinematic sequence cant play, main menu is sluggish and short freeze on win/loss

title: Grey Goo
notes: Grey Goo did start after initial crash and re-start. Cinematic sequence will play under 1 FPS. In-game menu is slow to initialize but after that will be responsive as it should be. Could not start the campaign due to the cinematic issue, but started the skirmish game and it played normally with barely noticeable stutter.

title: Grey Goo
notes: Cutscenes don't work. Managed to get past it by bashing keys, played the first mission of the campaign, the game crashed when I finished the main objective.

title: Grey Goo
notes: Crashes after start, sents error to the devs but doesn't give any information on it directly to me.

title: Grey Goo
notes: Menus are slow to respond, cinematic is a black screen, gameplay itself is pretty solid.
The start of the game, lvl and main menu is (very) slow as well as saving. But otherwise the game works fine. Just if you have patience in the manues you can play without problems

title: Grey Goo

notes: Working but too slow + freezes
title: Grey Goo

notes: Loading times are unusually high and the menus are sometimes unresponsive for long amounts of time. Gameplay is fine.
title: Grey Goo

notes: Gets through the first few loading screens, but freezes at main menu with only cursor responsive.
title: Grey Goo

notes:
had to RENAME/REMOVE video file in data (movies.meg) this allows the game to operate, simply pressing ESCAPE at any video cutscene's normal playback at this point would resume normal loading.
this also seemed to load at "decent" speeds...
"avatar portraits" also did not load in missions (e.g surak),  instead greyed out. that could be the fault of movies.meg though...
performance was lousy, dropped settings to minimum, actually had to exit entirely as it did not flush the gpu for the new settings.
i'm ballparking around 25fps. with dips to maybe 15.  (no fps counter)
that said, the game's core seems mostly operational, i'd bet finding out why wine's d3d11 works is the best bet to going to a gold or platinum rating.
title: Grey Goo

notes: Game works okay, but fmv's stop after few frames and have to be skipped.
